nav.app_mode_tip: Switch between the Simple and Advanced views. Advanced adds the Tags and Timeline tabs and the Settings → Data tools.
banner.dev_mode: Maintainer mode — all pages are visible. Run `mm web` without `--dev` for the polished surface.
banner.model_error: Model failed to load — check Settings.
banner.emb_mismatch: Embedding mismatch — {details}. Search may not work until resolved.
home.orientation.title: Getting started
home.orientation.intro: Three steps to start using memtomem.
home.orientation.step_add: Add your notes, docs, and code.
home.orientation.step_connect: Sync your skills, subagents, and commands out to Claude Code, Codex, and more.
home.activity.summary_aria: {total} chunks across {active} active days. {last7} chunks in the last 7 days. Most active day: {date}, {count} chunks.
home.activity.summary_sample_aria: Activity map is based on the {limit} most recent chunks returned by the timeline query. Sample covers {active} active days. Most active sampled day: {date}, {count} chunks.
home.activity.cell_aria_one: {date}, {count} chunk, {intensity}. Click to view in Timeline.
home.activity.cell_aria_other: {date}, {count} chunks, {intensity}. Click to view in Timeline.
home.action.reindex_title: Open Index and enable Force re-index. Indexing does not start until you run it.
home.action.export_title: Open Settings to the Export / Import section
home.action.dedup_title: Open Settings to the Deduplicate section. The scan does not start automatically.
home.state.no_sources_hint: Add files from the Index tab
home.state.no_pinned: No pinned chunks yet — click ☆ Pin in the detail panel
home.pin.missing_title: Pinned chunk is missing. Press Remove to clear it.
search.help: Semantic search across your indexed memories. Filter by tag, namespace, type, or relevance.
search.filter_toggle_title: Show/hide tag, type, sort and other filters
search.view_title: Switch between card and compact list view
search.tag_filter_title: Comma-separated tags to include in results
search.relevance_tooltip: How closely this result matches your query.
search.ns_filter_title: Limit search to a specific namespace
search.type_filter_title: Filter by chunk type (markdown, code, etc.)
search.topk_help: Maximum results returned. Increase for broader coverage.
search.topk_title: Maximum number of results to return
search.context_title: Include N neighboring chunks around each result for more context
search.adv_title: Source multi-select, score threshold, saved searches, export
search.adv_score_help: Minimum similarity score (0–1). Higher = more relevant but fewer results.
search.tags_empty_hint: No tags — type below to add
glossary.units: Sources are the files you add. Chunks are the searchable pieces. Memories are everything together.
glossary.namespace: A namespace groups related memories — by project, agent, or runtime — so you can scope a search to one set.
glossary.token: Tokens are how models count text — roughly three-quarters of a word each. Chunk size is measured in tokens.
glossary.mcp: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the open standard memtomem uses to expose memory tools to AI assistants.
glossary.ttl: TTL (time-to-live) is how long an entry is kept before it expires, in minutes.
glossary.search_pipeline: BM25 finds keyword matches, Dense finds meaning-based matches, RRF merges both into one ranking, and Final is the list after reranking and filters.
welcome.title: Welcome to memtomem
welcome.intro: Search your indexed memories by meaning — not just exact keywords.
search.empty_hint: / focus · j/k navigate · p pin · c copy
search.detail_empty: Select a result to view details
search.detail_save_title: Save content and tag changes to this chunk
search.detail_reset_title: Discard unsaved edits and restore original
search.detail_source_title: Show all chunks from the same source file
search.detail_pin_title: Pin this chunk to prevent decay removal
sources.help: Indexed source files. Click a file to browse chunks. Drag the divider to resize. Sort by name, chunks, size, or recency.
sources.title: Sources
sources.filter_placeholder: Type to filter by path or body… e.g. .md, src/, utils
sources.filter_title: Filters source list by partial path or indexed body match
sources.empty_text: Select a source to browse its chunks
sources.language_drift_banner: ⚠️ {count} summaries don't match your language setting ({current})
index.help: Three ways to add memories to memtomem.
index.mode.folder_desc: Scan an existing directory in place.
index.mode.upload_desc: Copy files into memtomem uploads, then index them.
index.mode.compose_desc: Write a new note directly into your memtomem memories.
index.folder_oneshot_hint: One-shot scan. For automatic re-indexing, use
index.force_unsafe_title: Index files even if they contain secrets (API keys, tokens). Audit-logged. Git-tracked project_shared files are still blocked.
index.register_source_title: Add this path to memory_dirs so future changes are auto-indexed
index.add_privacy_hint: Privacy guard scans for tokens, API keys, and private keys — not email or phone numbers.
compose.privacy_warning_message: What looks like an API key was found. This content will be stored as-is in the local database and exposed to search — proceed anyway?
confirm.redaction_blocked_message: The server detected {hits} secret-like pattern(s) on {surface}. Bypass and write anyway? This will be audited.
toast.redaction_bypassed: Bypassed redaction guard ({hits} hits) — entry written.
toast.redaction_bypass_partial: Bypass partial — {succeeded} of {total} blocked file(s) written. Check per-file results below.
toast.upload_redaction_cancelled: Upload cancelled — redaction guard left {count} file(s) blocked.
settings.nav.ctx_wiki_tip: Your personal git library of canonical skills, subagents, and commands — canonical = the one authoritative copy each project pulls a pinned snapshot from via `mm context install` (it never renders into a project on its own). It lives at ~/.memtomem-wiki; to move it to another machine, push and pull it yourself with ordinary git. The wiki is optional — your projects keep working without it.
settings.nav.ctx_wiki_dirty_tip: Uncommitted wiki edits — not yet reachable by `mm context install` (it installs committed git objects only). Commit them with `mm wiki commit` — or, in dev mode, the Wiki section's Commit button — to let projects pull them.
settings.ctx.primer: memtomem keeps your master copies in one Store (.memtomem/) and Syncs them out to your Runtimes — Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, and other detected tools. Import pulls a runtime's copy back in. It's one-way: edit in the Store, then Sync.
settings.ctx.projects_fetch_failed: Project list failed to load — falling back to Server CWD only ({error})
settings.ctx.flow_aria: The Store syncs one-way out to your detected runtimes.
settings.ctx.runtimes_glossary: The AI tools memtomem syncs to — Claude Code, Codex, Kimi, and others detected on this machine.
settings.ctx.last_synced_tooltip: Reflects the most recent stored-file mtime, not a recorded sync event. Editing a stored file without Sync All also bumps this.
settings.ctx.wiki_behind_tip: The wiki has newer commits than {n} installed asset(s) in this project. Update them from the Wiki section, or run `mm context update --all`.
settings.ctx.matrix_sync_disabled_title: No runtime sync for local drafts or missing projects
settings.ctx.matrix_sync_not_enrolled_ti: Not active — activate this project on the Projects board to sync it
settings.ctx.matrix_sync_paused_title: Sync paused — resume it on the Projects board
settings.ctx.error_sync_paused: Project sync is paused — resume it on the Projects board.
settings.ctx.error_sync_not_enrolled: Project is not active for sync — activate it on the Projects board.
settings.ctx.error_no_memtomem_store: The destination project isn't a memtomem store yet — run "mm context init" in it first.
settings.ctx.cascade_unavailable_hint: Sync is paused or not active for this project, so only the stored copy is deleted — runtime files are left in place.
settings.ctx.move_copy_tooltip: Move or copy this artifact to another store or project
settings.ctx.move_copy_mcp_title: Copy MCP server to another project
settings.ctx.move_copy_mcp_note: MCP servers have a single store: the copy keeps the name and lands in the destination project's shared store.
settings.ctx.move_copy_mcp_no_dest: No other eligible project to copy to. Activate a destination project from the Projects portal first.
settings.ctx.move_copy_collision: The destination already has an artifact with this name. Rename it or choose another destination.
settings.ctx.move_copy_shared_confirm_ti: Write to the shared project store?
settings.ctx.move_copy_shared_confirm_me: This saves your master copy into the project's shared store — committed to git, so your team will see it.
settings.ctx.wiki_desc: Browse the global wiki (~/.memtomem-wiki) — a separate git library, not your ~/.memtomem user store or config — of canonical skills, subagents, and commands. Install snapshots an item into the current project's .memtomem/ store.
settings.ctx.wiki_dev_tip: Read-only in production. In dev mode you can seed vendor overrides, edit canonical and override files, and commit — all in the browser.
settings.ctx.wiki_empty_hint: Run `mm wiki init` to create ~/.memtomem-wiki, then reload.
settings.ctx.wiki_no_assets_hint: Add skills, subagents, or commands with the mm wiki CLI.
settings.ctx.wiki_diff_none: No override seeded for this vendor.
settings.ctx.wiki_seed_hint: Renders the canonical baseline into the wiki working tree — review it, then use the Commit button or run mm wiki … commit (no manual git needed).
settings.ctx.wiki_reseed_confirm_msg: Re-seed the {vendor} override for {type} "{name}"? Your current override is kept as a .bak sibling.
settings.ctx.wiki_install_hint: Wiki edits change ~/.memtomem-wiki, a separate git library shared across projects. Install snapshots this asset into the current project's .memtomem/ store; it does not edit ~/.memtomem/config.json or the user-level ~/.memtomem/<artifact>/ store. Update refreshes an installed one to wiki HEAD.
settings.ctx.wiki_force_confirm_msg: {type} "{name}" has local edits in {project}. Force-update overwrites them — each edited file is kept as a .bak sibling.
settings.ctx.wiki_update_unchanged: {type} "{name}" is already up to date in {project}
settings.ctx.wiki_already_installed: {type} "{name}" is already installed — use Update to refresh
settings.ctx.wiki_not_installed: {type} "{name}" is not installed — use Install first
settings.ctx.wiki_install_privacy_blocke: The privacy scan blocked this: a secret was detected in the wiki bytes of {type} "{name}". Remove it from the wiki, then retry.
settings.ctx.wiki_override_hint: Edit this vendor's override in place. Save writes the file and leaves the wiki uncommitted; then use the Commit button (it appears after you Save) or run mm wiki … commit.
settings.ctx.wiki_override_busy: Wiki is busy — another sync is in progress. Try again.
settings.ctx.wiki_override_privacy_warn: Saved {vendor} override — but {count} possible secret(s) were detected; review before committing.
settings.ctx.wiki_override_conflict_msg: This override changed on disk since you opened it. Reload to discard your edits, or Force save to overwrite (a .bak sibling is kept).
settings.ctx.wiki_canonical_hint: Edit the base canonical — every vendor renders from this. Save writes the file and leaves the wiki uncommitted; then use the Commit button (it appears after you Save) or run mm wiki … commit. Subagents and commands must still parse, or the save is rejected.
settings.ctx.wiki_canonical_busy: Wiki is busy — another sync is in progress. Try again.
settings.ctx.wiki_canonical_parse_failed: Canonical not saved — it does not parse: {error}
settings.ctx.wiki_canonical_privacy_warn: Saved canonical — but {count} possible secret(s) were detected; review before committing.
settings.ctx.wiki_canonical_conflict_msg: This canonical changed on disk since you opened it. Reload to discard your edits, or Force save to overwrite (a .bak sibling is kept).
settings.ctx.wiki_commit_summary: Commit {count} saved change(s) to {asset} into the wiki's git history.
settings.ctx.wiki_commit_nothing: Nothing to commit — the saved bytes already match HEAD.
settings.ctx.wiki_commit_head_moved: The wiki moved on disk since you loaded it. Your edits are still on disk — re-save them, then commit again.
settings.ctx.wiki_commit_target_changed_: A file changed on disk since you saved it. Commit the current on-disk bytes anyway?
settings.ctx.wiki_commit_busy: Wiki is busy — another operation is in progress. Try again.
settings.ctx.wiki_commit_privacy_warn: Committed — but {count} possible secret(s) were detected in the message.
settings.ctx.confirm_delete_msg: This will remove the stored artifact from .memtomem/.
settings.ctx.confirm_cascade_codex: This will also delete files in ~/.codex/ (user-scope). Continue?
settings.ctx.confirm_sync: Push {count} {type} to the runtimes configured for this store?
settings.ctx.confirm_sync_all: Push all stored artifacts in {dest} to the runtimes configured for this store?
settings.ctx.confirm_sync_impact: This will create {create} missing and overwrite {overwrite} out-of-sync runtime files in: {runtimes}.
settings.ctx.confirm_sync_settings_impac: Plus create {create} and overwrite {overwrite} settings files.
settings.ctx.confirm_sync_counts: This will create {create} missing and overwrite {overwrite} out-of-sync runtime files.
settings.ctx.confirm_sync_overwrite_warn: {overwrite} runtime file(s) that drifted out of sync will be overwritten.
settings.ctx.confirm_import_overwrite_hi: Enable Overwrite to replace the existing ones.
settings.ctx.sync_lock_timeout: Sync didn't run — another process is holding a destination lock. Try again in a moment.
settings.ctx.sync_skipped_attention: Sync finished, but {count} item(s) were skipped: {items}
settings.ctx.import_priority: When the same name exists in more than one runtime, the first copy found is imported, scanning runtimes in a fixed order (Claude first, then Antigravity; skills also import from Codex and Kimi). Other runtimes are export-only and never read back.
settings.ctx.conflict_msg: Someone else modified this file while you were editing. Choose how to resolve.
settings.ctx.conflict_draft_restored: Restored unsaved edits from previous session
settings.ctx.status_legend: In sync — nothing to do. Out of sync — Sync to update the runtime. Not in runtime — Sync to add it. Not yet imported — Import to pull it into the Store.
settings.ctx.simple_toggle_tooltip: Switch between the simplified overview and the full advanced controls.
settings.ctx.simple_toggle_aria: Toggle the simplified Context Gateway view
settings.ctx.simple_verdict_action: Some items aren't in your tools yet — sync to push them out.
settings.ctx.simple_verdict_attention: Some items need your attention — open Advanced to review.
settings.ctx.simple_verdict_empty: Nothing is stored for this project yet.
settings.ctx.status_simple_not_saved: In a tool, not saved here
settings.ctx.simple_empty_hint: Open Advanced to add items, import from a tool, or switch project or store.
settings.ctx.parse_error_hint: Fix the file at {path}, then click Refresh.
settings.ctx.pointer_missing_target: Run Sync All to push {count} entries that are not in a runtime yet.
settings.ctx.pointer_missing_canonical: {count} runtime entries are not in the store yet — open {leaf} to import.
settings.ctx.pointer_empty: Nothing here yet — open {leaf} to create or import.
settings.ctx.pointer_empty_hooks: No hooks stored yet — open {leaf} to adopt hooks from your runtimes.
settings.ctx.pointer_empty_mcp: No MCP server definitions yet — open {leaf} to create one.
settings.ctx.tier_glossary: Where a copy lives in the Store and how far it reaches: User (every project on this machine), Project (shared) (committed to git, shared with the team), or Project (local) (a gitignored draft, never pushed).
settings.ctx.no_artifacts_hint: Create one or import from existing runtimes.
settings.ctx.scopes_load_failed: Couldn't load project scopes — retry, and check the server logs if it persists.
settings.ctx.diff_tab_hint: Click the Diff tab to load...
settings.ctx.draft_stash_failed: Draft could not be saved locally — keep this tab open until you save.
settings.ctx.tier_tooltip_user: ~/.memtomem/ — applies to every project on this machine
settings.ctx.tier_tooltip_project_shared: This project's .memtomem/ — committed to git, shared with the team
settings.ctx.tier_tooltip_project_local: Gitignored local drafts — never pushed to runtimes
settings.ctx.empty_hint: Place {type} under {canonical}/<name>/ then click Sync, or click Import to pull existing {type} from {scan_dirs} within this project.
settings.ctx.empty_hint_user: Place {type} under {canonical}/<name>/ and run `mm context sync --scope user`. User {type} are shared across all projects.
settings.ctx.empty_hint_mcp: Place {type} under {canonical}/<name>/ then click Sync. MCP servers sync to this project's .mcp.json — there is no Import step.
settings.ctx.empty_hint_mcp_project_only: MCP server definitions exist only in the Project (shared) store — switch the Stored-in filter to Project (shared) to manage them.
settings.ctx.sync_empty_canonical: No stored {type} under {canonical}. Create one first.
settings.ctx.runtime_only_banner: {count} {type} found in {scan_dirs}; none imported yet. Click Import to bring them into memtomem.
settings.ctx.sync_disabled_tooltip: Nothing to push yet — create or import {type} first.
settings.ctx.sync_all_disabled_tooltip: Nothing to push yet — create or import artifacts in each section first.
settings.ctx.sync_all_nothing_synced: Nothing to sync yet — every section is empty. Create or import artifacts first.
settings.ctx.sync_all_not_enrolled_toolt: The selected project is not active for sync — activate it on the Projects board.
settings.ctx.sync_all_paused_tooltip: The selected project's sync is paused — resume it on the Projects board.
settings.ctx.missing_canonical_project_s: Not yet imported into the project store (.memtomem/)
{count} {type} exist in {scan_dirs} but are not in memtomem's store yet. Click Import above, or run:
settings.ctx.missing_canonical_user_titl: Not yet imported into your user store (~/.memtomem/)
settings.ctx.missing_canonical_user_body: {count} {type} exist in your runtimes but are not in memtomem's user store yet. Click Import above (you'll confirm the host paths first), or run these commands:
settings.ctx.missing_canonical_mcp_body: {count} {type} exist in {scan_dirs} but are not in memtomem's store yet. There is no Import for MCP servers — open an entry to view its definition, then add it with Create.
settings.ctx.missing_canonical_project_l: Not yet imported into local drafts
{count} {type} exist in your runtimes but are not in your local drafts yet. Local drafts are gitignored and never pushed to runtimes — initialize them with the commands below:
settings.ctx.project_local_no_fanout_too: Project (local) is a draft store — drafts are never pushed to runtimes.
settings.ctx.write_blocked_user_tooltip: Project (shared)-only action — switch the Stored-in filter to Project (shared).
settings.ctx.write_blocked_project_local: Project (local) is a draft store — switch to Project (shared) to publish to runtimes.
settings.ctx.write_blocked_user_banner: User store — writes land outside this project (~/.memtomem, ~/.claude, …) and ask for confirmation before touching disk.
settings.ctx.write_blocked_project_local: Draft store — local drafts stay in .memtomem/ and are never pushed to runtimes.
settings.ctx.host_write_confirm_message: This User-store action writes {count} path(s) outside the project root:
settings.ctx.force_unsafe_message: {count} file(s) look like they may contain a secret (e.g. an API key). If you've reviewed them and they're safe — for example a type annotation like 'api_key: str' — you can import anyway:
settings.ctx.force_unsafe_sync_message: {count} file(s) look like they may contain a secret (e.g. an API key). If you've reviewed them and they're safe — for example a type annotation like 'api_key: str' — you can sync them to your runtimes anyway:
settings.ctx.privacy_blocked_shared_hint: Shared (project) imports can't override a flagged secret — git history is permanent. Open the skill and use 'Import to user library' instead, or remove the flagged line.
settings.ctx.privacy_blocked_shared_sync: Shared (project) sync can't override a flagged secret — git history is permanent. Remove the flagged line, then re-run sync.
settings.ctx.versions.title: Versions
settings.ctx.versions.freeze_tooltip: Freeze the current stored content as an immutable version
settings.ctx.versions.promote_tooltip: Point the selected label at this version (rollback = pick an older version)
settings.ctx.versions.empty: No versions yet — freeze the current stored content to create v1.
settings.ctx.versions.migrate_required: This artifact uses flat layout, which has no version store. Enable versioning to convert it to directory layout.
settings.ctx.versions.enable_tooltip: Convert this flat artifact to directory layout so it can be versioned
settings.ctx.versions.enable_success: Versioning enabled for {name} — freeze the current stored content to create v1.
settings.ctx.versions.list_chip_tooltip: Label {label} → version {tag}. Manage versions in the detail panel.
settings.hooks.detail.click_hint: Click a rule to view details.
settings.ctx.overview_desc: Pick a section in the sidebar to import what you already have, or create something new — then sync it out to Claude Code, Codex, and your other detected runtimes.
settings.ctx.refresh_tooltip: Refresh detected Claude Code / Codex / Kimi runtime status
settings.ctx.sync_all_tooltip: Push memtomem's stored artifacts to the runtimes configured for this store
settings.ctx.sync_all_aria: Sync all artifacts to the configured runtimes
settings.ctx.skills_desc: Reusable workflows stored in .memtomem/skills/ and synced to each runtime's skills folder (.claude/skills/ etc.).
settings.ctx.commands_desc: Project slash commands stored in .memtomem/commands/ and synced to each runtime's commands folder (.claude/commands/ etc.).
settings.ctx.agents_desc: Specialized subagents stored in .memtomem/agents/ and synced to each runtime's agents folder (.claude/agents/ etc.).
settings.ctx.mcp_servers_desc: MCP server definitions stored in .memtomem/mcp-servers/ and synced to the project's .mcp.json. Project-scoped only — a single store, with no user-level copy.
settings.ctx.add_project_prompt: Absolute path to a project root (e.g. /Users/me/Edu/inflearn):
settings.ctx.add_project_warning_no_runt: No .claude/.gemini/.agents/.kimi/.memtomem directory found under this root.
settings.ctx.portal_desc: Browse, switch, rename, and unregister the projects memtomem tracks. Health shows which roots are missing or not yet initialized.
settings.ctx.portal_hidden_uninit: {count} uninitialized project(s) hidden — uncheck "Initialized only" to show them.
settings.ctx.portal_no_match_filtered: No projects match "{query}" with the "{runtime}" filter.
settings.ctx.portal_enroll_tip: Activate this project so it syncs
settings.ctx.portal_stale_tip: This folder has no .memtomem store yet — run mm context init inside it to start tracking.
settings.ctx.portal_add_project_tooltip: Register a project folder so memtomem can track it
settings.ctx.portal_add_project_aria: Add a project to the portal
settings.ctx.runtime_uninstalled_tooltip: Not installed (Click to view install guide)
settings.ctx.guide_claude_desc: Claude Code supports user, local, or project-local configuration scopes.
settings.ctx.guide_claude_note: Or run with "-s user" to register globally for all projects.
settings.ctx.guide_antigravity_desc: Register memtomem in Antigravity CLI (agy) or Antigravity IDE (VS Code).
settings.ctx.guide_codex_desc: Codex CLI reads MCP servers from ~/.codex/config.toml (TOML format).
settings.ctx.guide_kimi_desc: You can automatically register memtomem for Kimi CLI using the CLI wizard.
settings.ctx.runtime_error_tooltip: Could not read this client's config
settings.ctx.confirm_remove_project: Stop tracking "{label}" ({root})? Files on disk are unaffected.
settings.ctx.scope_experimental_tip: Discovered via the opt-in ~/.claude/projects scan; the path may be misdecoded.
settings.ctx.skills_add_project_tooltip: Register a project folder so memtomem can detect its skills
settings.ctx.skills_import_tooltip: Pull existing skill definitions from a detected runtime into memtomem
settings.ctx.skills_sync_tooltip: Push memtomem's stored skills to the runtimes configured for this store
settings.ctx.skills_sync_aria: Sync skills to the configured runtimes
settings.ctx.commands_add_project_toolti: Register a project folder so memtomem can detect its commands
settings.ctx.commands_import_tooltip: Pull existing commands from a detected runtime into memtomem
settings.ctx.commands_sync_tooltip: Push memtomem's stored commands to the runtimes configured for this store
settings.ctx.commands_sync_aria: Sync commands to the configured runtimes
settings.ctx.agents_add_project_tooltip: Register a project folder so memtomem can detect its subagents
settings.ctx.agents_import_tooltip: Pull existing subagent definitions from a detected runtime into memtomem
settings.ctx.agents_sync_tooltip: Push memtomem's stored subagents to the runtimes configured for this store
settings.ctx.agents_sync_aria: Sync subagents to the configured runtimes
settings.ctx.mcp_servers_add_project_too: Register a project folder so memtomem can manage its .mcp.json
settings.ctx.mcp_servers_add_project_ari: Add project for MCP server definitions
settings.ctx.mcp_servers_create_tooltip: Create a new project MCP server definition
settings.ctx.mcp_servers_sync_tooltip: Push stored MCP server definitions to project .mcp.json
settings.ctx.mcp_servers_sync_aria: Sync MCP server definitions to project .mcp.json
settings.hooks.desc: Lifecycle hooks run a command on events like a tool call or session end, stored in .memtomem/settings.json and applied to the active runtime only.
settings.hooks.title: Hooks
settings.hooks.sync_now_tooltip: Apply .memtomem/settings.json hook config to the active runtime
settings.hooks.sync_now_disabled_no_sour: No .memtomem/settings.json to sync — create it first via `mm init`.
settings.hooks.sync_now_disabled_no_hook: .memtomem/settings.json has no hook rules to sync.
settings.hooks.pending: hooks will be added on sync
settings.hooks.delete_confirm: Delete "{label}" from the selected target settings file?
settings.hooks.promote_confirm_private: Save "{label}" to .memtomem/settings.json? This can be committed and shared with teammates.
settings.hooks.promote_all_confirm: Save {count} target-only hook rule(s) to memtomem-managed settings?
settings.hooks.promote_all_confirm_priva: Save {count} target-only hook rule(s) to .memtomem/settings.json? These can be committed and shared with teammates.
settings.hooks.promote_delete_original_o: Remove original from the selected target after saving
settings.hooks.promote_delete_success: Saved to memtomem and removed the original target rule
settings.hooks.promote_delete_partial: Saved to memtomem, but the original target rule was not removed.
settings.hooks.promote_all_result: {saved} saved, {deleted} original(s) removed, {conflicts} conflict(s), {aborted} stale, {failed} failed
settings.hooks.original_already_removed: Original target rule is already gone
settings.hooks.promote_conflict: memtomem already has a different hook rule for this event and matcher.
settings.hooks.edit_unavailable_v1_hint: Direct editing is not available here yet. Edit {path} manually.
settings.hooks.rule_action_stale: Hook rule changed. Refresh and retry.
settings.hooks.no_source_hint: Create .memtomem/settings.json or run mm init to set up hooks.
settings.hooks.error_hint: Fix the JSON file and reload.
settings.hooks.no_hooks_hint: Add hook rules to .memtomem/settings.json to make them appear as pending or synced.
settings.hooks.duplicate_banner_one: 1 memtomem-managed hook entry already exists in the {tier} target ({path}) — run {cmd} to move it. Active target: {active}.
settings.hooks.duplicate_banner_many: {count} memtomem-managed hook entries already exist in the {tier} target ({path}) — run {cmd} to move them. Active target: {active}.
settings.config.desc: Server settings: embedding provider, storage backend, search and indexing parameters.
settings.config.guide_text: Select a config section to see its documentation.
settings.ns.desc: Organize memories by namespace and review chunk counts per group.
settings.ns.title: Namespaces
settings.ns.empty.title: No namespaces yet
settings.ns.empty.body: Index files with a namespace, or define one in your config rules to bucket memories.
settings.ns.help.summary: What do these namespace labels mean?
settings.ns.help.intro: Namespaces label where each memory came from so you can scope a search by origin. They are memtomem indexing labels — they do not replace each tool's own memory model.
settings.ns.help.default: Memories you add directly, with no namespace set.
settings.ns.help.claude: Claude Code per-project auto-memory, bucketed by the project folder above memory/.
settings.ns.help.codex: Codex memories, split into codex:rollout_summaries (session recaps), codex:extensions (ad-hoc notes), and codex:global (everything else).
settings.ns.help.gemini: Antigravity / Gemini global memory (~/.gemini/GEMINI.md), imported one-shot via mm ingest gemini-memory.
settings.ns.help.agent: A single agent's isolated scope during multi-agent runs.
settings.ns.help.foot: Mapping rules come from mm init or the Config tab.
settings.dedup.desc: Find near-duplicate chunks by similarity threshold and merge or skip them.
settings.dedup.preflight: Scan previews candidates only. Merge buttons appear on reviewed pairs and delete only the duplicate chunk you choose.
settings.dedup.threshold_help: Similarity above which chunks are considered duplicates. Default 0.92 catches near-identical content.
settings.dedup.empty_text: Run Scan to see duplicate candidates
settings.dedup.scan_timeout: Scan timed out (120 s). Try a smaller Max Scan.
settings.dedup.summary_one: Found {count} candidate pair. Review each and choose which chunk to keep.
settings.dedup.summary_other: Found {count} candidate pairs. Review each and choose which chunk to keep.
settings.decay.desc: Expire chunks older than a cutoff date, optionally filtered by source path.
settings.decay.preflight: Run Scan first. Expire stays disabled until the current filters have a reviewed candidate count.
settings.decay.preview_required: Run Scan again before expiring. The filters changed since the last preview.
settings.reset.desc: Permanently delete all memory data and reinitialize the database. Embedding config is kept.
settings.reset.warning_text: This will permanently delete ALL data (chunks, sessions, history, relations, entities, etc.) and reinitialize the database. Embedding configuration is preserved.
settings.reset.backup_hint: Export a JSON bundle first if you may need to restore this database later.
settings.reset.confirm_message: This will permanently delete ALL data. This action cannot be undone. Are you sure?
settings.sessions.desc: Browse episodic session history and per-session event timelines.
settings.sessions.title: Episodic Sessions
settings.scratch.desc: Ephemeral key-value scratch pad with optional TTL, promotable to long-term memory.
settings.scratch.title: Working Memory
settings.procedures.title: Procedures
settings.health.desc: Memory health report — coverage, dead chunks, session stats. Includes watchdog run.
settings.health.title: Memory Health
settings.health.watchdog_desc: Periodic background checks. Run manually to force an update.
settings.watchdog.title: Health Watchdog
settings.redaction.title: Redaction Stats
settings.redaction.desc: Process-lifetime counts from the secret-redaction gate — how many writes were passed, blocked, or bypassed (Index without privacy gate), per surface. Resets on restart.
settings.redaction.empty: No writes have hit the redaction gate yet this session.
settings.export.desc: Export filtered chunks to a JSON bundle or import a bundle to re-index.
settings.export.title: Export
settings.export.preflight: Preview the filters before downloading so the bundle size is explicit.
settings.export.preview_required: Preview the current filters before downloading.
settings.import.title: Import
settings.import.hint: Upload a memtomem_export.json bundle to re-index its chunks.
tags.help: Tag cloud sized by frequency. Click a tag to filter search. Auto-Tag analyzes content to suggest tags for untagged chunks.
tags.title: Tags
tags.autotag_desc: Analyze chunk content and extract keyword-based tags automatically.
tags.autotag_dryrun_help: Preview suggested tags without applying. Uncheck to apply immediately.
tags.manage_impact: This will affect {count} chunks across all scopes.
timeline.help: Activity timeline of indexed chunks. Heatmap shows daily activity. Toggle Chunks/Files view.
timeline.empty_text: Click Load to view the timeline
shortcut.back: Back to results list from detail
picker.title: Pick a folder
picker.outside: Outside the picker scope. Close and type the path to use it.
toast.pinned_chunk_missing: Pinned chunk no longer exists. Remove the stale pin.
toast.advanced_only_section: That view lives in Advanced mode — switch to Advanced (top-right) to open it.
toast.quick_action.open_index: Opened Index. Choose a source, then run indexing.
toast.quick_action.reindex_ready: Force re-index is selected. Review the source, then run indexing.
toast.quick_action.open_export: Opened Export / Import. Choose filters before previewing or downloading.
toast.quick_action.open_dedup: Opened Deduplicate. Review the threshold, then run the scan.
toast.stream_fallback: Streaming failed. Try the Index button instead.
toast.watchdog_disabled: Health watchdog is disabled. Set MEMTOMEM_HEALTH_WATCHDOG__ENABLED=true to enable.
toast.select_saved: Select a saved search to delete
toast.chunk_target_missing: Couldn't locate the linked chunk in this view.
toast.index_blocked: {count} file(s) skipped — possible secrets. Enable 'Index without privacy gate' to index them anyway.
toast.index_blocked_project_shared: {count} project_shared file(s) blocked — git-tracked secrets can't be bypassed. Remove the secret or move to a local scope.
toast.sync_partial_settings_needs_confir: Sync All complete except Settings — confirm host writes in the Settings panel.
toast.sync_scope_changed: Sync target changed during confirmation — sync cancelled. Re-run sync for the current target.
toast.sync_targets_unavailable: Sync cancelled — the server did not disclose which files the write would touch.
confirm.emb_reset_msg: WARNING: All indexed vectors will be deleted. New config: {provider}/{model} (dim {dimension}). Re-indexing required afterwards.
confirm.ns_delete_msg: Delete all chunks in namespace '{name}'? This cannot be undone.
confirm.chunk_delete_msg: This will permanently remove {lines} from the source file "{source}" and delete the chunk from the index. This cannot be undone.
confirm.bulk_delete_msg: {count} chunks will be permanently removed from source files and deleted from the index. This cannot be undone.
confirm.expire_msg: Permanently delete {count} expired chunk(s). This action cannot be undone.
confirm.hooks_replace_msg: Replace your "{label}" rule with memtomem's version?
confirm.hooks_sync_msg:
Merge .memtomem/settings.json hooks into these runtime settings files?
{targets}
settings.exclude_patterns.err_save_block: Fix the highlighted exclude patterns before saving
settings.supported_extensions.reindex_hi: New extensions take effect on next index. Existing directories need a re-index to pick them up.
settings.reset.title: Reset to default (preview; press Save to apply)
sources.memory_dirs.title: Memory Dirs
sources.source_delete_title: Delete indexed chunks for this file
sources.memory_dirs.files_empty: No indexed files in this directory yet
settings.memory_dirs.moved_notice: Memory directories are managed in the
confirm.memory_dir_remove_msg: Remove {path} from memory_dirs? The directory and its files on disk are not touched.
confirm.memory_dir_delete_chunks_label: Also delete {count} indexed chunks from this directory
settings.config.guide.embedding.title: Embedding
settings.config.guide.embedding.desc: Controls how text is converted to vector representations for semantic search.
settings.config.guide.storage.title: Storage
settings.config.guide.storage.desc: Database backend for storing chunks, vectors, and FTS index.
settings.config.guide.search.title: Search Pipeline
settings.config.guide.search.desc: Hybrid search: BM25 (keyword) + Dense (semantic) fused via Reciprocal Rank Fusion.
settings.config.guide.indexing.title: Indexing
settings.config.guide.indexing.desc: Controls how files are discovered, chunked, and stored as searchable units.
settings.config.guide.decay.title: Time Decay
settings.config.guide.decay.desc: Reduces search scores of older chunks over time, prioritizing recent information.
settings.config.guide.mmr.title: Maximal Marginal Relevance
settings.config.guide.mmr.desc: Diversifies search results by penalizing chunks too similar to already-selected ones.
settings.config.guide.namespace.title: Namespace
settings.config.guide.namespace.desc: Organize chunks into logical groups for scoped search and management.
settings.config.guide.embedding.item.pro: ollama (local, free) or openai (cloud, paid). Determines which API to call for embeddings.
settings.config.guide.embedding.item.mod: nomic-embed-text (768d, English-optimized), bge-m3 (1024d, multilingual) etc. Must match Provider.
settings.config.guide.embedding.item.dim: Vector dimension. Must match the model output. Changing this requires re-indexing all data.
settings.config.guide.embedding.item.bas: API endpoint. Ollama: http://localhost:11434. OpenAI: https://api.openai.com/v1 (or compatible endpoint).
settings.config.guide.embedding.item.bat: Number of texts embedded per API call. Higher = faster indexing but more memory. Default 64.
settings.config.guide.embedding.item.api: Required for OpenAI provider. Not needed for local Ollama. Masked in UI for security.
settings.config.guide.embedding.item.thr: ONNX Runtime intra-op thread cap for the local fastembed provider. Default 4 — caps ONNX so a bulk reindex stays responsive on the same machine. Set to 0 to opt back into ORT default (all physical cores) for maximum throughput on dedicated machines, or any other small integer for finer control. Ignored for ollama/openai. Restart required.
settings.config.guide.storage.item.backe: Currently only sqlite is supported. Single-file DB, no external dependencies.
settings.config.guide.storage.item.sqlit: Full path to the database file. Contains all chunks, embeddings, FTS index, and metadata.
settings.config.guide.storage.item.colle: Logical table name for storing chunks. Change to use separate storage within the same DB.
Number of results returned by default. Can be overridden per query.
settings.config.guide.search.item.retrie: Toggle retrievers independently. BM25 = exact keyword matching (FTS5). Dense = semantic vector similarity.
settings.config.guide.search.item.candid: BM25/Dense Candidates control how many results each retriever fetches before fusion. Higher = better recall but slower.
Smoothing constant for rank fusion. Lower (10-30) = top ranks dominate. Higher (60-100) = more uniform blending.
settings.config.guide.search.item.rrf_we: Balance slider between BM25 and Dense. Center = equal weight. Left = keyword heavier. Right = semantic heavier.
settings.config.guide.search.item.tokeni: FTS5 tokenizer for keyword search. unicode61: built-in (all languages). kiwipiepy: Korean morphological analysis.
settings.config.guide.indexing.item.exte: File types recognized for chunking: .md, .py, .js, .ts, .tsx, .jsx, .json, .yaml, .yml, .toml. Edit inline; changes persist immediately.
settings.config.guide.indexing.item.excl: Glob patterns skipped during indexing. Built-in secret/noise groups are read-only; user patterns are editable inline.
settings.config.guide.indexing.item.max_: Upper bound for chunk size. Long sections are split to stay under this limit.
settings.config.guide.indexing.item.targ: Soft target the chunker aims for between Min and Max. Most chunks land near this size; Max is the hard cap.
settings.config.guide.indexing.item.min_: Short chunks below this threshold are merged with their neighbor. 0 = no merging.
settings.config.guide.indexing.item.over: Token overlap between adjacent chunks. Adds shared context at boundaries for better retrieval. 0 = no overlap.
settings.config.guide.indexing.item.stru: For JSON/YAML/TOML files. "original": extracts raw text lines per key (preserves formatting, line numbers). "recursive": serializes via json.dumps, recursively splits deep nested structures by sub-keys.
settings.config.guide.decay.item.enabled: When active, search scores are multiplied by a time-based decay factor. Newer chunks rank higher.
settings.config.guide.decay.item.half_li: Days until decay factor reaches 0.5. A 30-day half-life means month-old chunks score ~50% of fresh ones.
settings.config.guide.mmr.item.enabled.t: When active, results are re-ranked after retrieval to reduce redundancy.
settings.config.guide.mmr.item.lambda.te: Balance between relevance and diversity. 1.0 = pure relevance (no MMR effect). 0.0 = max diversity. Default 0.7.
settings.config.guide.namespace.item.def: Applied when no namespace is specified during indexing or memory add. Leaving this as "default" results in untagged chunks (backward compatibility — chunks indexed before namespaces existed).
settings.config.guide.namespace.item.aut: When enabled, derives namespace from the parent folder name during indexing. Overrides Default NS.
settings.config.guide.embedding.howto.su: Set the env vars, restart, then reset embeddings and re-index.
settings.config.guide.embedding.howto.wa: Changing model/dimension invalidates all existing vectors. Must reset + re-index after restart.
settings.config.guide.storage.howto.summ: Set the SQLite path env var, restart, then re-index the new DB.
settings.config.guide.search.howto.title: Tune Search Quality
settings.config.guide.search.howto.summa: Adjust weights and candidates, then Save — changes apply live.
settings.config.guide.search.howto.warn: Changing tokenizer to kiwipiepy requires: pip install kiwipiepy. Falls back to unicode61 if unavailable.
settings.config.guide.indexing.howto.sum: Edit inline or Save chunk settings, then re-index existing data.
settings.config.guide.indexing.howto.war: Extensions / Exclude Patterns / chunk size changes only affect data indexed after the change. Re-index to apply to existing chunks.
settings.config.guide.decay.howto.title: Enable Decay
settings.config.guide.decay.howto.summar: Enable, set a half-life, Save — then scan to expire stale chunks.
settings.config.guide.mmr.howto.title: Enable MMR
settings.config.guide.mmr.howto.summary: Enable, tune Lambda, Save — lower Lambda for more diverse results.
settings.config.guide.namespace.howto.su: Set a Default NS or enable Auto NS, then Save.
settings.config.guide.namespace.howto.wa: Auto NS takes priority over Default NS. Existing chunks keep their namespace until re-indexed.
settings.config.reload_invalid: Config file invalid on disk: {error} — fix it (or run `mm init --fresh`) before saving from the UI.
settings.config.reload_external: Config file changed externally — reloaded from disk.
settings.config.reindex_both: Tokenizer and chunk settings changed. FTS index rebuild and re-indexing recommended for full effect.
settings.config.reindex_fts: Tokenizer changed. FTS index rebuild is recommended so existing data uses the new tokenizer.
settings.config.reindex_chunk: Chunk settings changed. Re-indexing is recommended to apply new chunking to existing files.
settings.config.select.structured_mode.o: Extract original text lines per key, split large sections by line count
settings.config.select.structured_mode.r: Serialize via json.dumps, recursively split by sub-keys
